newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Today's -4.2% slide (to $72.23) extends a giveback of the ~24% post-Q2-FY26-earnings run; the key catalysts (Q2 beat with $944M revenue / $1.15 non-GAAP EPS, $1B+ multi-generational Android RF design win, Qorvo merger notes-exchange progress) all predate June 6. Cited pressure points are mobile customer concentration, costs, and merger/regulatory risk - none of which touch the AI-bottleneck thesis these pages rank for.

sources.label: Skyworks (SWKS) up 24.2% since last earnings report - Yahoo Finance
Skyworks and Qorvo to combine - Skyworks IR press release
Skyworks Q2 beat and long-duration design win - Simply Wall St

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already flagged SWKS for removal on weak direct HBM/packaging/AI-networking relevance, and nothing since June 6 is new; today's -4.2% is a mobile/merger-flow giveback, not AI-node evidence, so hold at the bottom with mild negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Broad Markets (42% of sales, +10% YoY incl. WiFi, data center, auto) plus the Qorvo combination could give the merged $22B RF/analog platform more credible edge-AI and connectivity content over time.
verdicts.neutral: A competently executing RF house with a fresh Android design win, but its AI-node exposure remains indirect and small relative to dedicated HBM, advanced-packaging and networking names; rank 92 already reflects that.
verdicts.bear: Mobile concentration, merger integration/regulatory risk, and zero bottleneck criticality in the AI compute chain mean the name is dead weight on this list regardless of cheapness.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Qorvo merger close and synergy capture; any concrete data-center/edge-AI content wins from the combined portfolio.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Mobile customer concentration and merger regulatory/integration risk with no offsetting AI-bottleneck exposure.

verdicts.rankRationale: The CPO/optical thesis is 'limited data-center optical exposure' and nothing since June 6 changes that; yesterday's remove call stands, and a -4.2% flow-driven pullback in an RF/mobile name does not create CPO alpha, so hold with slight drift down.
verdicts.bull: Post-pullback valuation (~$10.8B, well off the post-earnings high) is undemanding, and the Qorvo merger creates scale that could eventually fund data-center RF/connectivity adjacencies.
verdicts.neutral: Solid Q2 execution and a $1B+ Android design win are real, but they are handset economics - irrelevant to the co-packaged-optics bottleneck this page ranks; current rank 76 is already generous.
verdicts.bear: No meaningful CPO, optical or data-center cable content; holding it on this list dilutes the alpha screen while mobile concentration and merger risk dominate the actual stock story.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Qorvo merger completion and any announced data-center/optical-adjacent product roadmap from the combined company.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued post-rerating giveback in a mobile-concentrated name with no CPO exposure to anchor it on this page.
